seoTitle: Burlong vs Burma Road — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Burlong and Burma Road. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Burlong vs Burma Road: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Burlong and Burma Road.
heading: Burlong vs Burma Road — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Burlong, Western Australia and Burma Road, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Burma Road has a lower overall climate risk score (39/100) compared to Burlong (41/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Burlong or Burma Road safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Burlong has a flood risk score of 34/100, while Burma Road scores 50/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Burlong scores 46/100 for bushfire risk. Burma Road scores 63/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Burlong or Burma Road?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Burma Road has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.67% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
